name: nub
description: TypeScript-first developer supertool — a Rust CLI that augments your Node.js
comment: oxc-transform, oxc-parser, and @oxc-project/runtime are pinned to an EXACT version (no caret) and MUST share the same version — they are one oxc release. They determine transpile EMIT (and module-format detection), and NUB_VERSION is the sole transpile-cache-busting key (runtime/preload.mjs). The cache invariant — 'the transpiler version is a pure function of the nub version' — requires that oxc cannot float without a deliberate nub version bump. The caret range allowed silent in-range float at build time (release.yml uses --no-frozen-lockfile); pinning exact closes that. Bump all three together, in lockstep with `make version`. See wiki/runtime/transpile-cache.md.
